data.title: Ridgetech Announces 1-for-150 Reverse Share Split to Take Effect April 7, 2026
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest update is out from Ridgetech ( (RDGT) ).

Ridgetech, Inc., a wholesale distributor of pharmaceutical and healthcare products in China listed on Nasdaq under the ticker RDGT, announced on April 3, 2026 that it will execute a 1-for-150 reverse share split of its ordinary shares. The move will reduce the number of outstanding ordinary shares from 134,835,960 to approximately 898,906 as of April 7, 2026, with no change to shareholders’ proportional ownership other than minor adjustments from rounding up fractional shares.

The reverse split, which was approved by Ridgetech’s board and shareholders, is scheduled to take effect on April 7, 2026 at 5:00 p.m. Eastern Time, after which trading will continue on the Nasdaq Capital Market on a split-adjusted basis under the existing symbol RDGT. By consolidating its share count and increasing the per-share price, the company aims to strengthen its capital markets profile and maintain compliance with Nasdaq listing standards, a step that could be significant for investor perception and market liquidity.

The most recent analyst rating on (RDGT) stock is a Hold with a $0.02 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Ridgetech stock, see the RDGT Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on RDGT Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, RDGT is a Underperform.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance (ongoing operating losses, revenue decline, and still-elevated leverage despite some 2025 improvement) and bearish technical momentum (negative MACD with low RSI). Valuation provides limited support due to a negative P/E and no dividend yield data.

To see Spark’s full report on RDGT stock, click here.

More about Ridgetech

Ridgetech, Inc. is a Cayman Islands–incorporated wholesale distributor of pharmaceutical and other healthcare products in China. The company operates both online and offline, trading on major national e-commerce distribution platforms as well as its own proprietary platform to broaden nationwide reach for drug and healthcare product distribution.

data.title: Enviri Sets Vote on $3.04 Billion Clean Earth Sale and Breakup Plan
data.description:
New activity is brewing for Enviri ( (NVRI) ). The company has submitted a Form DEFM14A to the SEC, indicating an upcoming shareholder vote. This form gives shareholders all the information they need to develop an informed vote. Read on to learn more about the proposed company action.

End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Enviri Corporation has called a virtual special meeting of shareholders on May 4, 2026 to vote on a series of transactions tied to the $3.04 billion cash sale of its Clean Earth business to France’s Veolia Environnement. The deal structure includes a holding company merger, a reorganization separating Clean Earth from the Harsco Environmental and Harsco Rail units, and the creation of “New Enviri” to own the remaining businesses.

Under the plan, existing Enviri shareholders will receive one share of New Enviri for every three shares they hold after the internal merger, with New Enviri becoming a separate public company. Immediately after this distribution, Veolia will acquire the Clean Earth holding entity, and shareholders will receive cash of at least $14.50 and up to $16.50 per share in the merger, with part of the proceeds earmarked for debt repayment and to support large European rail contracts.

The board unanimously determined the transaction is fair and in the best interests of shareholders and is urging investors to vote in favor of approving the merger agreement, the related executive compensation on an advisory basis, and the right to adjourn the meeting to solicit more votes if needed. The outcome will determine whether Enviri can complete the separation and sale, reduce leverage, and reposition its remaining operations while delivering a significant cash payout to investors.

The most recent analyst rating on (NVRI) stock is a Sell with a $18.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Enviri stock, see the NVRI Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on NVRI Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, NVRI is a Neutral.

The score is held down primarily by weak financial performance (declining revenue, ongoing net losses, and materially higher leverage) and only partially offset by positive operating cash flow. Technicals also suggest near-term weakness (low RSI and trading below short-term moving averages). Earnings call guidance is constructive on operational initiatives but remains cautious due to Rail losses, high interest burden, and modest/negative free cash flow expectations.

To see Spark’s full report on NVRI stock, click here.

More about Enviri

Enviri Corporation is a Delaware-based industrial company whose operations include the Harsco Environmental and Harsco Rail segments. The group has also operated the Clean Earth environmental services business, which it has now agreed to sell, positioning its remaining operations as a standalone public company focused on environmental and rail-related services.

Average Trading Volume: 1,431,726

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $1.62B

For a thorough assessment of NVRI stock, go to TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

General Enterprise Ventures ( (CITR) ) has shared an update.

On April 1, 2026, CitroTech Inc. entered into a Transition Agreement under which Chief Technology Officer Stephen Conboy resigned effective March 31, 2026, and moved to an external advisory role to the CEO through June 30, 2026. During this 90-day period, he will not participate in internal management and will focus on transitioning relationships and intellectual property in exchange for monthly compensation, expense reimbursement, and product advances.

Following the transition period, Conboy will hold exclusive rights to sell certain CitroTech products around North Lake Tahoe, South Lake Tahoe, and Truckee, California, subject to minimum sales thresholds, preferred pricing, and audit rights. The agreement also provides for potential equity transactions, restricted-share royalties tied to revenue milestones, a future affiliate commission arrangement, and comprehensive releases, confidentiality, non-disparagement, and restrictive covenants, indicating an orderly leadership change without internal disputes while preserving long-term commercial ties with a key former executive.

More about General Enterprise Ventures

CitroTech Inc. is a technology company that develops proprietary products and systems, with a focus on inventions that can be commercialized in defined geographic territories. The company’s model includes leveraging strategic advisors and specialized distribution rights to expand product sales while maintaining control over pricing, territory performance, and equity ownership structures.

data.title: Flux Power Faces Loan Default, Liquidity Risks Mount
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Flux Power Holdings ( (FLUX) ) just unveiled an update.

On March 31, 2026, Flux Power Holdings, Inc. disclosed that it had failed to meet the minimum EBITDA covenant for the trailing three-month period under its existing Loan and Security Agreement with Gibraltar Business Capital, LLC, triggering an event of default on approximately $6.5 million of outstanding borrowings. The lender has kept the company’s credit line available during ongoing negotiations for an amendment or waiver, but it retains the right to restrict access or accelerate repayment, creating near-term liquidity and financing risk for Flux Power and heightening uncertainty for its stakeholders.

While Flux Power has successfully renegotiated this loan agreement in the past and remains optimistic about reaching a new accommodation, there is no assurance that new terms or a waiver will be obtained on favorable conditions or at all. If Gibraltar Business Capital exercises its default remedies, it could terminate further commitments, demand immediate repayment, and enforce its rights as a secured creditor, potentially straining the company’s operations and financial flexibility.

The most recent analyst rating on (FLUX) stock is a Hold with a $0.98 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Flux Power Holdings stock, see the FLUX Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on FLUX Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, FLUX is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak TTM fundamentals (ongoing losses and negative operating/free cash flow) and bearish technicals (below all major moving averages with negative MACD). A low P/E and a notably improved quarter (first profitability, margin/expense progress and product/software roadmap) provide some support, but near-term guidance risk from a major customer capital freeze and low cash keep overall risk elevated.

To see Spark’s full report on FLUX stock, click here.

The company’s activities and growth are closely tied to maintaining access to asset-based lending and meeting related financial covenants.

data.title: Forian to Go Private in CEO-Led Takeover Deal
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from Forian ( (FORA) ) is now available.

On April 2, 2026, Forian entered into a merger agreement under which a consortium of investors led by Chairman and CEO Max Wygod, alongside other senior executives and existing stockholders, will acquire all outstanding common shares not already owned by them for $2.17 per share in cash. The all-cash deal, which values the company’s equity at about $68 million and represents a roughly 22.6% premium to its unaffected August 22, 2025, share price, will be executed via a tender offer followed by a merger that takes Forian private, removing its stock from Nasdaq while keeping its Newtown, Pennsylvania headquarters, management team and Forian brand in place, with closing targeted for the second quarter of 2026 subject to a minimum tender and other customary conditions.

The transaction was unanimously approved by Forian’s board, following a special committee review with independent financial and legal advisors, and is fully backed by equity commitments from the investor consortium, eliminating financing risk. Governance terms include caps on company transaction expenses, a no-shop with limited fiduciary outs, and a $1.5 million termination fee plus expense reimbursement in certain scenarios, underscoring a tightly structured take-private process that secures a near-term liquidity event for public shareholders while consolidating control with existing insiders.

The most recent analyst rating on (FORA) stock is a Hold with a $2.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Forian stock, see the FORA Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on FORA Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, FORA is a Neutral.

The score is primarily supported by reduced financial risk from an essentially debt-free balance sheet and improved 2025 free cash flow, but it is held back by ongoing losses and an unhelpful negative P/E. Technical indicators add a neutral-to-slightly-weak backdrop with no clear momentum signal.

To see Spark’s full report on FORA stock, click here.

More about Forian

Forian Inc. is a data analytics and information solutions provider focused on traditional and emerging life sciences, healthcare payers and providers, and the financial services industry. The company specializes in acquiring, integrating, normalizing and commercializing large-scale healthcare data assets to help customers optimize and measure operational, clinical and financial performance.

In a letter accompanying new investor materials, Credit Acceptance’s CEO, who took the role in November 2025 after four years on the board, outlines a strategy centered on maximizing long‑term intrinsic value per share and Economic Profit while maintaining strict pricing discipline. The company is accelerating technology and AI investments in originations, data infrastructure, dealer‑facing tools, and servicing to sharpen risk assessment, reduce friction, and improve scalability, signaling a push to modernize operations for the next decade.

Its business model emphasizes disciplined underwriting, long‑term intrinsic value creation, and close alignment with both dealers and consumers, particularly in underserved markets where reliable transportation supports employment and financial stability. Bloomia Holdings ( (TULP) ) has issued an announcement.

On April 3, 2026, Bloomia Holdings, Inc. announced preliminary results of a rights offering that began in February 2026 and expired on April 1, 2026, after a brief extension to increase stockholder participation. Eligible stockholders of record as of February 16, 2026, received non-transferable rights to buy additional common shares at $4.05 each, including an over-subscription privilege subject to availability and proration.

Based on preliminary figures, the offering is expected to generate about $12.1 million in gross proceeds, with roughly $5 million in cash and $7.1 million from conversion of existing debt, leading to the issuance of approximately 3 million new shares, pending final tabulation and settlement. Bloomia plans to use the net cash proceeds primarily to repay acquisition debt at more than a 50% discount, a move that materially reduces leverage, cuts annual interest expense, and strengthens the balance sheet, which management says should support future growth and enhance long-term shareholder value.

The most recent analyst rating on (TULP) stock is a Hold with a $4.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Bloomia Holdings stock, see the TULP Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on TULP Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, TULP is a Neutral.

The score is weighed down primarily by high financial risk (heavy leverage and ongoing cash burn) despite improving operating performance. Technical indicators are neutral-to-weak with oversold signals but limited momentum confirmation, and valuation is constrained by negative earnings and no dividend support.

To see Spark’s full report on TULP stock, click here.

More about Bloomia Holdings

Bloomia Holdings, Inc. is a specialty agriculture company focused on making and managing agricultural investments in the U.S. and internationally. The Nasdaq-listed firm is the majority owner of Bloomia, one of the largest producers of fresh-cut tulips in the United States, giving it a significant presence in the floriculture and value-added agri-products market.

SES S.A. ( (FR:SESG) ) has provided an announcement.

On April 2, 2026, SES held its Annual General Meeting of shareholders in Betzdorf, Luxembourg, where investors approved all resolutions recommended by the board, including the 2025 annual accounts and a total annual dividend of EUR 0.50 per A-share and EUR 0.20 per B-share. The payout includes an interim dividend already distributed on October 16, 2025, and a final dividend scheduled for April 16, 2026, underscoring continued capital returns to shareholders.

Shareholders also backed governance measures, setting the board size at nine directors, re-electing Frank Esser and Anne-Catherine Ries, and appointing Joseph Cohen for a three-year term. The board re-elected Esser as chairperson and confirmed Ries and Peter van Bommel as vice-chairpersons for one year, reinforcing leadership continuity as SES advances its multi-orbit strategy and integration of Intelsat to drive verticalization and long-term value creation.

data.title: United Homes Group Amends Credit Facilities Amid Merger Plans
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

United Homes Group ( (UHG) ) has provided an update.

On March 31, 2026, United Homes Group, Inc. amended its Wells Fargo-led credit facility, securing temporary waivers of its debt service coverage and leverage ratio covenants until the earlier of May 31, 2026 or the occurrence of certain unrelated events of default. The amendment also obliges the company to fully refinance and repay this facility within 60 days if its pending merger with Stanley Martin Homes, LLC does not close by the outside date or is formally terminated.

On the same date, the company amended its Kennedy Lewis credit facility, waiving the debt service coverage and leverage ratio requirements for the period from January 1, 2026 through the fiscal quarter ending on or before March 31, 2026. That agreement likewise requires full refinancing and repayment within 60 days if the merger fails to close by the outside date, underscoring the extent to which United Homes Group’s capital structure and covenant relief are now closely tied to the completion of the Stanley Martin transaction.

The most recent analyst rating on (UHG) stock is a Sell with a $1.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on United Homes Group stock, see the UHG Stock Forecast page.

Village Farms International ( (VFF) ) has shared an announcement.

On March 30, 2026, Village Farms International’s long-serving Chief Financial Officer, Stephen Ruffini, notified the company that he intends to transition out of the CFO role as part of a planned succession process. He will remain in the position until a new CFO is appointed, and the company said his departure from the finance role is not linked to any disputes or concerns over financial reporting, accounting, operations, or policies.

Announced publicly on April 3, 2026, the succession plan will see Village Farms commence an immediate search for a new CFO while retaining Ruffini in a new executive post focused on mergers and acquisitions. The move underscores the company’s strategy to pair organic growth with accretive global acquisitions, building on Ruffini’s 17-year tenure in which he helped transform Village Farms into a leading cannabis CPG operator, complete key financings, and execute strategic transactions that supported its expansion and shareholder value.

More about Village Farms International

Village Farms International, Inc. is a global leader in cannabis and plant-based consumer packaged goods, leveraging decades of Controlled Environment Agriculture expertise and Dutch farming practices. The company operates extensive greenhouse and indoor cultivation assets, with major cannabis operations in Canada, participation in the regulated cannabis program in the Netherlands, and a large U.S. hemp-derived wellness platform.

Its Canadian operations include one of the world’s largest EU-GMP certified cannabis facilities and a leading portfolio of dried-flower brands such as Pure Sunfarms Pink Kush. Beyond cannabis, Village Farms runs a Clean Energy division that converts landfill gas into renewable natural gas and holds an equity stake in Vanguard Food LP, which is pursuing strategic acquisitions to build a North American branded food platform.

Innate Pharma S.A., a clinical-stage biotechnology company specializing in cancer immunotherapies and next-generation antibody therapeutics, is advancing a pipeline that includes IPH4502 for solid tumors, lacutamab for T cell lymphomas, and monalizumab in non-small cell lung cancer through collaborations with partners such as AstraZeneca and Sanofi. The company, headquartered in Marseille with a U.S. base in Rockville, is listed on both Euronext Paris and Nasdaq, underscoring its global reach in immuno-oncology.

Leveraging antibody engineering and novel target identification, it is advancing a pipeline of next-generation antibody therapeutics, including Nectin-4 ADC IPH4502 for solid tumors, lacutamab for cutaneous and peripheral T cell lymphomas, and monalizumab, partnered with AstraZeneca, for non-small cell lung cancer.

The company operates through collaborations with major biopharmaceutical partners such as Sanofi and AstraZeneca, as well as academic institutions, to drive innovation in immuno-oncology.
